channel.title: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded
channel.description:
<!--
Are you asking about "how to solve a specific issue/error/problem in your Frontity project"?
If so, please check this guide on the information we suggest you include in your topic so the community (and the Frontity team) can provide the best possible help as quickly and as efficiently as possible
https://community.frontity.org/t/about-the-dev-talk-questions-category/2/3
The info you may provide: - Description of your issue - Specific errors you’re getting in the terminal or in the browser’s console - A repository with the code of your project - A deployed version of your site - The URL of your WP REST API
-->
Hey guys,
I have one remaining issue with my frontity deployment.
Error steps to reproduce: 1. Navigate to any page like https://address/* 2. Router tries to redirect you to https://address/*/ -> notice the trailing slash! 3. Error is thrown
![Error screenshot|417x127](upload://4gUMEqmgTIUvc5r8VDhVv9A3Fb3.png)
What actually happens: You get redirected but no content is loaded anymore (I presume source.get bails)
Directly navigating to https://address/*/ does not cause any issues!
Environment: - production - cmd: yarn frontity serve - "frontity": "^1.14.3" - "@frontity/tiny-router": "^1.3.2", - "@frontity/core": "^1.11.1", -> was broken with 1.10 also
Reproducible: yes, deterministic, only on built version, not in dev-mode
Live address: Sorry, do no want to disclose this here, I can however send a private message to someone of the team.
Do you have any ideas how I can further debug this error?
Thank you very much so far for frontity. Saved me a lot of pain with stupid wordpress editors and injecting masses of custom scripts.
Best Regards

item.title: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded
item.dc:creator: Eiden
item.description:
Hey guys,

for anyone checking this thread:

The issue was actually that I had a package-lock.json in my repository and using npm in my deployment Dockerfile while using yarn otherwise. That means the issue was caused by version conflicts.

I appologize for not finding that before posting.

Read full topic
item.source: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded

item.title: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded
item.dc:creator: Eiden
item.description:
I read that, that is why I stated that I cannot disclose the url and asked only for further steps to debug, not a solution. I will try to make a minimum viable repo and deploy it somewhere so everyone can see it but that is going to take much time. You can close/delete the topic for now if you like if my topic is not satisfactory.

Read full topic
item.source: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded

item.title: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Hi @Eiden

Please have a look at the “Get Help” section of our Forum User’s Guide

Eiden:

Sorry, do no want to disclose this here, I can however send a private message to someone of the team.

To be able to help you with your issue the proper resources and information should be provided so any member of the community can reproduce the issue and get a better understanding of it.

Read full topic
item.source: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded

item.title: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded
item.dc:creator: Eiden
item.description:
Hey guys,

I have one remaining issue with my frontity deployment.

Error steps to reproduce:

  1. Navigate to any page like https://address/*
  2. Router tries to redirect you to https://address/*/ → notice the trailing slash!
  3. Error is thrown

What actually happens:
You get redirected but no content is loaded anymore (I presume source.get bails)

Directly navigating to https://address/*/ does not cause any issues!

Environment:

  • production
  • cmd: yarn frontity serve
  • “frontity”: “^1.14.3”
  • “@frontity/tiny-router”: “^1.3.2”,
  • “@frontity/core”: “^1.11.1”, → was broken with 1.10 also

Reproducible:
yes, deterministic, only on built version, not in dev-mode

Live address:
Sorry, do no want to disclose this here, I can however send a private message to someone of the team.

Do you have any ideas how I can further debug this error?

Thank you very much so far for frontity. Saved me a lot of pain with stupid wordpress editors and injecting masses of custom scripts.

Best Regards

Read full topic
item.source: Router issues with trailing slash - Range Error: Maximum call stack size exceeded
